title: notelaea linearis
intro: Notelaea linearis, also known as the narrow-leaved black peery or narrow-leaved black peery, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. The leaves of the Notelaea linearis plant have been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, where they are valued for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves have been used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Notelaea linearis can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the leaves in hot water or a solvent to extract its medicinal properties.
